target.name: Agility Robotics
target.founder: Jonathan Hurst, Damion Shelton, Mikhail Jones

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $385M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $2.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Divergent
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series E), post-money $2.3B

data.narrative:
Agility Robotics, a Series E robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jonathan Hurst, Damion Shelton, and Mikhail Jones, has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable, including peers like Divergent ($2.3B), Exotec ($2.0B), and Locus Robotics ($2.0B).

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage. The stage-progression method uses the company's $385M total raised as a base and applies a 1.4x Series E step-up multiplier, implying a $539M valuation. The peer-median approach anchors to three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $2.0B and a range of $2.0B to $2.3B. This peer set includes Divergent (Series E, $2.3B), Exotec (Series D, $2.0B), and Locus Robotics (Series F, $2.0B).

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $458M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.3B. The wide spread reflects tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors to capital deployed, and peer comparables, which suggest substantially higher valuations for robotics firms at this maturity. Key uncertainties include revenue run-rate relative to peers, path to unit economics, and the timing of subsequent financing. The peer-median approach carries more weight given the size and stage profile of the comparable set.
